rationale_top3: Strong nature and location appeal with proximity to Cable Beach, one of Australia's most iconic beaches Good cleanliness standards and well-maintained facilities throughout the park Solid value proposition with decent entertainment options for a caravan park in this premium location
rationale_honourable: Reasonable site sizes adequate for most caravans and camping setups, though not the most spacious
water_fun: Outstanding resort-style pool, shaded and clean
kids_play: Playground small, run down, not suitable young children
best_for: Families who want Cable Beach access and an amazing pool but can compromise on site space and should request family zones away from worker accommodation.
executive_summary:
PARK: Cable Beach Caravan Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-20
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 115

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Reviews consistently highlight an exceptional "resort style" pool described as "the best pool in Broome" and "better than Crown Towers," but the playground is "small, run down and not usable for young children." No organized kids activities or entertainment programs mentioned. Pool is the sole family entertainment feature.

Nature (16/20) — Excellent scores for mature shade trees throughout ("beautifully shady," "lots of beautiful big trees"), walking distance to famous Cable Beach (8-10 minutes), and "beautiful plants and birds." Wildlife sightings mentioned. Loses points as no bush setting or creek access beyond beach proximity.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Mixed feedback with recent reviews noting decline. Positive mentions of "very clean," "amenities always clean and tidy" balanced against "kitchen and bathrooms were a bit dirty," "bandaids on ground in showers," "dead cockroaches," "toilets blocked for whole stay," and "camp kitchen has used old rubbish everywhere." Inconsistent standards.

Value (10/15) — Significant concerns with "$73-80 per night" described as "biggest rip off," "way too expensive," "greedy," and "overpriced." Some found it "reasonably priced" and "half the price" of competitors. Value diminished by small sites charged same as large, inconsistent pricing, and dated amenities for premium rates.

Site Size (6/10) — Major weakness with repeated complaints: "squeezed in like sardines," "very small sites," "felt like we were on top of our neighbours," "packed to the rafters," "overcrowded," "narrow roads." Some large sites exist but allocation inconsistent. High-density 500-site park with tight maneuvering.

Sentiment (8/10) — Polarized responses. Many 5-star reviews praising staff and location ("came for 1 night stayed 2 weeks," "can't wait to come back"), but significant 1-2 star reviews citing poor management response, rude customer service, and declining standards. Repeat visitors note park has deteriorated over 6 years.

Location (9/10) — Excellent proximity to Cable Beach (walking distance), close to brewery, restaurants, pubs, and town (5 minutes drive). Bus stop at entrance. Perfect location for Broome attractions. Only detriment is no longer a shortcut through adjacent property to beach.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Site sizes are consistently too small for a premium-priced park, with families feeling "squeezed like sardines" and caravans "on top of neighbours" with inadequate space for rigs and vehicles spilling onto driveways.
2. The park mixes long-term workers and backpackers with holiday families, causing noise issues ("vehicles coming and going late at night and early morning"), hygiene problems in shared facilities, and an uncomfortable atmosphere that families report has significantly declined in recent years.
3. Cleanliness and maintenance standards are inconsistent with aging amenities needing upgrade, blocked toilets going unrepaired, dirty camp kitchens with inadequate facilities for 500 sites, and customer service failures where management "clearly couldn't care less" about legitimate complaints.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"best pool in Broome," "squeezed in like sardines," "should be renamed backpackers caravan park," "walking distance to Cable Beach," "beautifully shady sites," "playground small run down," "way too expensive," "standards have dropped," "amenities could get an upgrade," "small caravan/campervan sites," "came for 1 night stayed 2 weeks," "worst park we have stayed in"

rationale_top3: Outstanding cleanliness standards with universal praise for the new amenities block, described as 'spotlessly clean', 'best caravan park amenities ever', and 'cleaned daily' with exceptional facilities including massive toilet cubicles and thoughtful design features. Excellent value positioning as the 'cheapest price in the area' with unpowered sites at $42-44, consistently recognized as 'well priced overall' and 'best value' while maintaining high facility standards in the competitive Broome market. Perfect location with Cable Beach just 10-20 minutes walking distance, bus stop directly out front, and 'close to everything by foot' while maintaining a peaceful atmosphere away from the busy town centre.
rationale_honourable: Tarangau Caravan Park earns Silver recognition for its outstanding cleanliness standards and exceptional value positioning in the Broome market. The park's newly renovated amenities block receives universal praise, with multiple families noting the spotless facilities and thoughtful design details. While lacking dedicated kids' entertainment facilities, the park compensates with its peaceful atmosphere, shade-filled sites, and excellent walking distance to Cable Beach, making it ideal for families seeking a quieter, well-maintained base for exploring Broome's natural attractions.
kids_play: No playground; quiet park setting only
pet_detail: Pet-friendly with designated sites; strict 12-point pet policy requiring signature on arrival
best_for: Families with older children who can appreciate beach walks and quiet time, or those seeking a tranquil, budget-friendly alternative to larger resort-style parks while exploring Broome
executive_summary:
PARK: Tarangau Caravan Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-11
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 99

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (4/20) — No pool, playground, or organized kids activities mentioned; one review notes "live music and food trucks regularly on Friday nights" but no dedicated children's entertainment infrastructure; families praised the quiet atmosphere but several noted "missing a pool" as a downside for children

Nature (16/20) — Excellent natural setting with "lots of shade," "huge trees," "beautiful frangipani trees," and "mature trees" throughout; Cable Beach accessible via "15-20 min walk" with multiple reviews confirming easy beach access; park described as "looks like the Botanical Gardens" with well-maintained greenery; some sites have grassy areas though several reviews mention dusty/sandy patches

Cleanliness (15/15) — Outstanding cleanliness consistently praised: "spotlessly clean," "cleanest park I have stayed at," "amenities are in fantastic condition they are cleaned daily," "best caravan park amenities ever," "squeaky clean throughout"; new modern bathroom facilities repeatedly highlighted as exceptional; grounds "regularly raked" and "immaculately maintained"

Value (14/15) — Excellent value positioning: "cheapest price in the area," "well priced overall," "best value," "very reasonable price," "$42 per night for two people unpowered"; multiple reviews compare favorably to more expensive parks; only deduction for occasional complaints about cancellation policies and one review calling it "very expensive for a patch of red dust"

Site Size (8/10) — Generally good site sizes: "large sites," "good size sites," "spacious," "reasonably large," "heaps of room for your van"; some reviewers noted "some sites are a tad small" and one mentioned their "first site was a bit tight" requiring a change; variable site quality with some grassy and others bare dirt/sand

Sentiment (9/10) — Very positive overall sentiment with high recommendation rates: "would definitely stay again" appears frequently, "we kept extending our stay," "stayed for 2 months," "stayed here for 3 weeks"; strong repeat visitation indicated; only minor deductions for 4-5 very negative reviews citing aggressive management and poor customer service experiences

Location (10/10) — Perfect location score: "walking distance to Cable Beach," "15-20 min walk to beach," "bus stop right out front," "close to everything," "perfectly located"; easy access to town, restaurants, and Divers Tavern; Broome Explorer bus stop directly at entrance for those without vehicles

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of family entertainment facilities—no pool, no playground, no kids club, no organized activities—which significantly limits appeal for families with young children seeking structured entertainment during their stay.
2. Inconsistent site quality with some sites being bare dirt/sand rather than grass, creating dusty conditions in windy weather and reducing comfort for families setting up play areas for children.
3. Several credible reviews cite aggressive management behavior, strict policies (12-point pet agreement, inflexible refund policies), and incidents where guests were asked to leave for posting negative reviews, creating potential stress for families.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"best caravan park amenities ever," "spotlessly clean," "walking distance to Cable Beach," "cheapest price in the area," "family owned not a corporate park," "lots of shade," "very quiet park," "no pool," "management very aggressive," "friendly and helpful staff," "immaculately maintained," "15 min walk to beach"

rationale_top3: Outstanding pool facility consistently rated as one of the best in Australian caravan parks, providing excellent water recreation for families Spacious sites with mature shade trees and good room for large rigs, with many guests praising the generous allocation and concrete slabs Secure location just outside Broome town center offering safety and theft-free peace of mind while remaining accessible to beaches and attractions
rationale_honourable: Broome Caravan Park earns a Silver rating primarily for its outstanding pool facility, which reviewers consistently describe as one of the best they've encountered in caravan parks across Australia. The park offers spacious sites with good shade from mature trees, helpful and friendly staff who go above and beyond, and a secure location just outside of town that balances accessibility with safety. While amenities show their age and could benefit from updates, families appreciate the clean facilities, reasonable off-season pricing, and the relaxed atmosphere that makes it a solid choice for exploring the Broome region.
water_fun: Outstanding resort-style pool with lap lane available
kids_play: Minimal on-site entertainment, occasional food trucks only
pet_detail: Dog friendly park, pets must be kept on lead at all times
best_for: Budget-conscious families with their own vehicle who prioritize a fantastic pool and spacious sites over modern amenities and on-site kids' entertainment
executive_summary:
PARK: Broome Caravan Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-26
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 293

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Reviews indicate minimal on-site kids entertainment with occasional food trucks and live music, but no dedicated playground or structured activities. Families appreciated the nearby speedway for occasional events, but most entertainment requires driving into town or to beaches.

Nature (12/20) — The park features lovely mature shady trees throughout and guests can see wallabies roaming the grounds. Located about 10 minutes from Cable Beach and Gantheaume Point, families have good access to Broome's natural attractions, though the park itself sits on a main road without direct beach or bushland access.

Cleanliness (10/15) — Reviews consistently mention that facilities are kept clean with daily maintenance, though multiple guests note amenities are "tired," "dated," and "showing their age." Some reviews mention cockroaches, crickets, and sewerage smells, while others praise spotless amenities, indicating variable experiences across different areas of the large park.

Value (11/15) — Off-season rates around $25-40/night receive praise as "fantastic value" and "cheapest in Broome," but peak season pricing ($60-80/night) draws criticism as expensive for the older facilities provided. Families appreciate the value compared to Cable Beach alternatives, though some felt amenities don't match the price point.

Site Size (8/10) — Reviews frequently mention "spacious," "large," and "huge" sites with good room for big rigs, though experiences vary significantly. Some guests received generous grassy sites with shade, while others were allocated cramped dirt sites close to roads or neighbors, suggesting inconsistent site allocation.

Sentiment (8/10) — Overall sentiment is positive with many return visitors and recommendations, though management changes appear to impact consistency. Recent reviews show mixed experiences with some guests extending stays multiple times while others left early. The pool is the consistent highlight across virtually all reviews.

Location (7/10) — Situated 5-10 minutes drive from Broome town center and beaches, the location receives mixed feedback. Many appreciate being "out of town so security is not the worry" and away from crowds, while others note it's too far to walk anywhere and taxi costs add up. Proximity to main road creates noise issues for some sites.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Amenities are consistently described as old, tired, and dated throughout the park, with bathrooms, camp kitchens, and cabins all requiring modernization to meet current caravan park standards.
2. Pool closes at 5:20-5:30pm daily, which reviewers find frustratingly early during Broome's hot summer months when families most want to cool off in the evening.
3. Minimal on-site entertainment or activities for children, with no playground equipment, kids' club, or structured activities mentioned, requiring families to drive elsewhere for entertainment.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"best pool in a park by far", "Pool is huge and amenities are great", "amenities are dated but clean", "out of town so security is not the worry", "Pool closes at 5.30pm what a joke", "spacious sites with concrete slab", "staff very helpful", "safe and we have been theft free"

rationale_top3: Outstanding location directly across from iconic Cable Beach with 400-500m walk to patrolled swimming and sunset viewing, plus convenient access to Divers Tavern, cafes and town amenities Well-maintained saltwater pool described as 'resort-like' and modern main amenities block praised as 'cleanest and best showers/toilets so far on our roadtrip' Established natural environment with shady trees throughout and wildlife sightings including blue tongue lizards, creating a pleasant bushland atmosphere near the beach
rationale_honourable: RAC Cable Beach Holiday Park earns Silver recognition primarily for its outstanding location directly across from Cable Beach, with families able to walk to the iconic beach and watch stunning sunsets. The park features a well-maintained saltwater pool that families love, modern amenities in the main block, and shady sites under established trees. While the park excels in location and has passionate staff members who go above and beyond, it falls short of Gold due to the complete absence of children's entertainment facilities (no playground despite multiple requests), extremely tight and difficult-to-navigate site access that frustrates families with larger rigs, and inconsistent customer service experiences that detract from what could otherwise be an excellent family destination.
water_fun: Saltwater pool, popular with families, closes early
kids_play: No playground or kids entertainment facilities available
pet_detail: Dog friendly with designated dog section, leash rules strictly enforced, separate smaller sites allocated for pet owners at same price as larger sites
best_for: This park is best suited for families with young children who want to be walking distance to Cable Beach and don't mind the absence of a playground, particularly those with smaller campervans or willing to accept tight site access in exchange for the premium beachside location.
executive_summary:
PARK: RAC Cable Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2025-06-10
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 164

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Reviews consistently highlight the complete absence of children's entertainment with multiple guests requesting a playground. One review states "just needs something for kids IE play ground / pillow / adventure play" and another notes "Es hat kein Spielplatz, obwohl in der Umgebung keiner zu finden ist." The pool offers some entertainment but closes at 6pm limiting evening use, and there's a small putt-putt golf facility next door but no on-site kids activities or adventure play equipment.

Nature (17/20) — Exceptional beach access with Cable Beach directly across the road, described as "2 minute drive" or "400m walk to the patrolled area." Multiple reviews praise walking to the beach for sunsets, with one stating "we were a 2 minute drive from the beach where we would meet mates, swim, have some drinks and dinner and watch the beautiful sunset which never got old." The park features established shady trees throughout and guests mention seeing "wildlife blue tounge, tata and frilled neck lizards." The natural sand dune environment and proximity to iconic Cable Beach delivers strong nature credentials.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Mixed feedback with newer amenities praised as "stunning" and "very clean" but older facilities criticized. Recent reviews note "the cleanest and best showers/toilets so far on our roadtrip" and "amenities are always very clean" for the main block. However, cabin cleanliness receives multiple complaints: "cabin was dirty with strands of long black hair," "kitchen was very greasy," "fridge was extremely dirty." One review states the amenities "need a good scrub to keep it looking new, it is getting dated and stained very quickly." Pool maintenance also criticized with hair left for days and chlorination issues.

Value (9/15) — Significant concerns about pricing with multiple reviews calling it "very expensive" and "overpriced." One family paid "$2,000 for 7 nights" in a cabin, another "$1400 for 4 nights" expecting better quality. Powered sites at "$80 per night" and "$65 for a sandy dusty campsite" viewed as excessive. Several reviews note better value available at competing parks nearby. The location premium is acknowledged but many feel the price doesn't match the experience, particularly given tight sites, lack of kids facilities, and inconsistent cabin quality. RAC membership benefits appear limited with members not receiving preferential treatment.

Site Size (5/10) — Overwhelmingly negative feedback about site size and access. Reviews repeatedly describe sites as "tight," "packed in," "squished in like sardines," and "the tightest sites we've seen in our 5 and a half years of travelling around Australia." Multiple guests struggled with access: "roads are barely wider than a van," "near impossible to reverse in," "took 20 minutes of manoeuvring to even get out." Smaller campers and rooftop vehicles allocated the worst sites despite same pricing. One review notes "our neighbour just for eg had to get the neighbours across from him to move their car out so he could move his van over." Trees create additional obstacles. Only positive mentions are for select larger sites when park is quiet.

Sentiment (8/10) — Polarized sentiment with passionate supporters and disappointed critics. Positive reviews show strong loyalty: "10/10 from us," "we have booked again for next year," "second time staying here and we will return." Multiple guests extended their stays. However, significant negative sentiment from families: "would never recommend it to anyone," "won't be coming back," "honestly it was not relaxing at all." Customer service issues with specific staff members (Maurice/Monica) generate strong negative reactions. The park maintains high occupancy and repeat bookings from grey nomads, but families express frustration with lack of child facilities and tight sites.

Location (10/10) — Outstanding location scores with universal praise for Cable Beach proximity. Reviews consistently highlight "amazing location right across the street from Cable Beach," "500 metre walk to Cable Beach," "just across the road from the famous cabel beach." Convenient access to Divers Tavern pub, general store with cafe, and bus stop directly outside. Walk to restaurants and amenities. "Everything is only a 10min drive away." The beachside position is the park's strongest asset with families able to easily access the patrolled beach, sunset viewing, and camel rides. Location alone drives many bookings despite other shortcomings.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of children's entertainment facilities — Multiple reviews from families specifically request a playground, adventure play, or any kids entertainment, with one noting "Es hat kein Spielplatz, obwohl in der Umgebung keiner zu finden ist und es auf dem Campingplatz genug Möglichkeiten da wären um einen zu erstellen," meaning there's no playground despite space being available and none in the surrounding area, which is a critical gap for a family holiday park.

2. Extremely tight and poorly designed site access — Reviews consistently describe the sites as "the tightest we've seen in 5 and a half years," with families taking "20 minutes of manoeuvring to even get out," roads "barely wider than a van," and neighbors unable to open awnings or needing others to move cars to maneuver, creating significant stress for families with larger rigs that contradicts the relaxation expected at a holiday park.

3. Inconsistent cleanliness and maintenance, particularly cabins — While the main amenities block receives praise, multiple cabin reviews detail "dirty kitchen utensils," "extremely dirty" fridges with sand, "strands of long black hair clinging to the bathroom floor," "pink mould all over" showers, and "filthy" rangehoods, indicating quality control issues that undermine the premium pricing and RAC brand standards families expect.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"amazing location right across the street from Cable Beach", "pool wonderful - feels like a resort", "just needs something for kids IE play ground", "tightest sites we've seen in our 5 and a half years of travelling", "cleanest and best showers/toilets so far on our roadtrip", "staff went above and beyond", "very expensive paid 2,000 for 7 nights", "no better caravan park in Broome"

rationale_top3: Security is a major, recurring issue with multiple reports of theft, break-ins, trespassers from the beach, and lack of effective fencing, cameras, or night patrols, making families feel unsafe. Value for money is poor, with families consistently calling the park overpriced ($90-$115/night) compared to the experience, especially given security and service issues. Customer service and communication are inconsistent, with reports of rude staff, unanswered phones, unhelpful reception, language barriers with backpacker staff, and management dismissive of guest concerns.
rationale_honourable: Discovery Parks Broome offers an exceptional oceanfront location with direct beach access, a splash park nearby, and fishing opportunities from the jetty. Families love the large, well-appointed sites, modern cabins, and refreshing pool with ocean views. While the park has significant appeal, recurring security concerns, variable cleanliness standards, and inconsistent customer service impact the overall family experience.
water_fun: Large refreshing pool with ocean views
kids_play: Nearby public splash park and playground
best_for: Families seeking an oceanfront holiday park base close to Broome's attractions, who prioritize location and are comfortable managing their own security.
executive_summary:
PARK: Discovery Parks - Broome
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-13
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 193

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (12/20) — Reviews mention proximity to the public splash park, playground, and jetty for fishing. Some families appreciated yoga, kids club, and food vans on-site, but many noted these activities were cancelled or inconsistent, especially off-season. Limited on-site entertainment compared to expectations.

Nature (17/20) — Oceanfront location with direct beach access, turquoise water views, and opportunities to see turtles, dolphins, stingrays, and fish from the jetty. Families enjoyed the natural setting, sunsets, and staircase to the moon viewing. However, swimming not recommended due to crocodile and stinger risk.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Mixed feedback. Many praised modern, clean facilities and spotless ensuites, while others reported cobwebs, dusty restrooms, poor drainage in showers, and backpackers from nearby car park using facilities, leaving them dirty. Cleanliness standards varied across different amenity blocks.

Value (8/15) — Widely criticized as expensive, with many calling it overpriced ($90-$115/night for powered sites). Families felt the price didn't match the experience given security issues, inconsistent service, and facility concerns. Some appreciated the location justified cost, but value concerns were dominant.

Site Size (8/10) — Generally positive about large, spacious sites with room for caravans, awnings, and setup. Beachfront and ensuite sites especially praised. Some complaints about uneven, sandy, or dirt sites, and tight entry/exit during renovations. Overall, decent space for larger rigs.

Sentiment (6/10) — Sentiment heavily polarized. Many loved the location, views, and staff, with repeat stays planned. However, significant negative sentiment around theft, security, inconsistent service, poor communication, and management attitude. Trust and safety concerns deterred repeat visits for many families.

Location (6/10) — Excellent proximity to Town Beach, markets, Matsos Brewery, jetty, and town (walkable or short drive). Central and accessible. However, recurring complaints about open access from beach allowing trespassers, lack of security fencing, and safety concerns in the area reduced location appeal.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Security is a major, recurring issue with multiple reports of theft, break-ins, trespassers from the beach, and lack of effective fencing, cameras, or night patrols, making families feel unsafe.
2. Value for money is poor, with families consistently calling the park overpriced ($90-$115/night) compared to the experience, especially given security and service issues.
3. Customer service and communication are inconsistent, with reports of rude staff, unanswered phones, unhelpful reception, language barriers with backpacker staff, and management dismissive of guest concerns.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"right next to the splash park, playgrounds, and the jetty", "great experience for families", "pool here is a standout", "recurring thefts", "anyone can walk into this site", "steady stream of backpackers", "extremely overpriced", "we felt very unsafe", "cleanest facilities", "gorgeous location, beautiful park", "great location easy walk into town", "security is absolutely dismal"

park_name: Broome's Gateway Pet Friendly Caravan Park
rationale_top3: No family facilities whatsoever - zero kids entertainment, no playground, no pool, no activities, and explicit reviews stating 'not family friendly' with surcharges for children over 12 Remote location 20-30km from Broome requiring daily drives to access town, beaches, and attractions, making it impractical for families wanting to explore the region No powered sites available, only generators allowed 8am-8pm creating noise issues, plus missing basic infrastructure like dump point, and inconsistent cleanliness with ant infestations reported
rationale_honourable: Broome's Gateway offers a bush-style camping experience with large, spacious sites and pet-friendly facilities including an off-leash area. While the park delivers excellent value compared to central Broome accommodation and maintains reasonably clean amenities, it lacks the entertainment, powered sites, and convenience that most families with young children require for an enjoyable holiday park stay.
water_fun: No pool or water play facilities
kids_play: No playground or kids entertainment mentioned
pet_detail: Very pet friendly with enclosed off-leash dog run area and pet sitting service available in air-conditioned office.
best_for: Self-sufficient families with pets who prefer spacious, natural bush settings and don't require on-site children's activities or entertainment.
executive_summary:
PARK: Broome's Gateway Pet Friendly Caravan Park
SCORE: 52/100
DATE SCORED: 2025
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 73

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (2/20) — Reviews consistently note this park has no entertainment facilities, playground, or kids' activities whatsoever; one review explicitly states "not family friendly" with additional charges for children over 12.
Nature (14/20) — Multiple reviews praise the "bush camping with amenities" feel, "country feel", "rural/station atmosphere", "beautiful waking up to the sound of birds", shady trees, and peaceful natural surroundings; however, some mention dusty red dirt sites with ants.
Cleanliness (11/15) — Most recent reviews praise "spotless" toilets and "clean amenities", though several older reviews mention ant infestations in camp kitchen, broken washing machines, and areas needing cleanup; facilities described as "dated but acceptable".
Value (8/15) — Highly polarized feedback: some praise "great price" at $36-40/night compared to Broome parks, while multiple families with children call it "expensive" and "absolute robbery" for unpowered sites with no dump point, especially with $20/night extra charges per child over 12.
Site Size (9/10) — Overwhelming consensus on "huge sites", "very spacious", "heaps of room between sites", "generous size", and "way bigger than any caravan park" with easy drive-through access.
Sentiment (5/10) — Mixed sentiment: self-contained couples and pet owners express high satisfaction and return intentions, while families with young children express disappointment; many note it was chosen only because Broome parks were full.
Location (3/10) — Consistently noted as 20-30km/20-30 minutes from Broome on bumpy dirt access road, requiring daily drives for tours and attractions; described as "inconvenient distance" for multi-night family stays.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of family entertainment, playground, pool, or children's activities makes this unsuitable as a destination park for families with young children seeking holiday experiences.
2. Location 20-30km from Broome requires daily driving for all attractions, tours, and town visits, with "terrible road in, so bumpy" access limiting convenience for families.
3. No powered sites (generators only 8am-8pm), no dump point, insufficient toilet facilities for park size during peak periods ("only six unisex shower cubicles" causing "unbearable" wait times), and additional charges for children over 12 significantly reduce value and convenience for families.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"not family friendly", "no power and no dump point", "20 minutes out of Broome", "very spacious sites", "bush camping with amenities", "beautiful waking up to the sound of birds", "very dog friendly even has an off leash pen", "very few toilets for such a large park", "old amenities but clean", "absolute robbery this place"
